# COURSE: Thực tập dự án tốt nghiệp (CSC10204)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC10204
- **English Name**: Capstone Projects
- **Vietnamese Name**: Thực tập dự án tốt nghiệp
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Assoc. Prof. Nguyễn Văn Vũ
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 7
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 0 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 150 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 180 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
Topics for the capstone project are proposed by either students or project suppervisors, and are approved by suppervisors. The topics can be either research-oriented or application-oriented. In the research-oriented project, students need to propose a relevant research topic (based on recommendations of a project supervisor), carry out necessary research activities, write a report, and present the project in front of a committee. The research work may lead to publications in a scientific conference or journal. In the application-oriented project, students in collaboration with a project supervisor propose and develop a software application, write a report to describe the application along with the process of developing it, and present the project in front of a committee. The application should have the potential to be applied in practice and is possibly deployed for real use.
Students on each team are assessed and graded by the committee using the course’s predefined rubrics. They are required to meet a designated reviewer to present their work and answer any questions the reviewer has. They then to present the project in front of the committee to defend their work. The final grade will be calculated from the grades given by the reviewer, the supervisor, and the committee members.
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Topic registration.
- Project proposal submission.
- Project report submission for defense.
- Project defense with the defense committee.
- Report revision and final submission after the defense.
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]The only grammar book you'll ever need: A one-stop source for every writing assignment,Thurman, Susan, and Larry Shea,2003,Adams Media
- [2]Writing for Computer Science (4th. ed.).,Justin Zobel,2014,Springer
- [3]How to write a great research paper,Simon Peyton Jones,,Microsoft Research, Cambridge
- [4]How to Write a SIGGRAPH Paper: A Guide to Choosing a Good Research Topic, Doing the Research, and Writing It Up,David Salesin, Adobe,2012,SIGGRAPH Tutorial

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Analyze a sufficiently complex problem, generate ideas and requirements for an appropriate solution to the problem
- G2.Apply appropriate knowledge, skills, techniques and tools to design, implement and evaluate the proposed solution (either a scientific solution or a software application)
- G3.Create a technical report to communicate the proposed solution in detail
- G4.Defend the project through a poster presentation to a reviewer and the committee
- G5.Practice project management activities
- G6.Practice teamwork
• Content:
- Topic registration.
- Project proposal submission.
- Project report submission for defense.
- Project defense with the defense committee.
- Report revision and final submission after the defense.
• Examination forms: Lab Report, Research Project
• Study and examination requirements:
- All students are responsible for reading and following strictly the regulations and policies of the school and university.

# COURSE: Thực tập tốt nghiệp (CSC10252)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC10252
- **English Name**: Industrial-based Thesis
- **Vietnamese Name**: Thực tập tốt nghiệp
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Assoc. Prof. Nguyễn Văn Vũ
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 7
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 0 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 250 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 300 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
The course is performed in the form of thesis projects. Each project is carried out by up to two students who are expected to complete the project within 6 months. The thesis project is expected to be the culmination of students’ years of undergraduate study. They need to apply all knowledge, skills, and experience gained from all courses that they have already taken to carry out work necessary to complete the thesis project. The project is also expected to be sufficiently large in size and complexity as compared with other projects taken during their undergraduate study.
The thesis project can be either research-oriented or application-oriented. In the research-oriented project, students need to propose a relevant research topic (based on recommendations of a thesis supervisor), carry out necessary research activities, write a thesis, and present the thesis in front of a thesis committee. The research work may lead to publications in a scientific conference or journal. In the application-oriented project, students in collaboration with a thesis supervisor propose and develop a software application, write a thesis to describe the application along with the process of developing it, and present the thesis in front of a thesis committee. The application should have the potential to be applied in practice and is possibly deployed for real use.
Students on each team are assessed and graded by the thesis committee using the course’s predefined rubrics. They are required to meet a designated thesis reviewer to present their work and answer any questions the reviewer has. They then to present the thesis in front of the committee to defend their work. The final grade will be calculated from the grades given by the reviewer, the supervisor, and the committee members.
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Topic registration.
- Project proposal submission.
- Project report submission for defense.
- Project defense with the defense committee.
- Report revision and final submission after the defense.
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]The only grammar book you'll ever need: A one-stop source for every writing assignment,Thurman, Susan, and Larry Shea,2003,Adams Media
- [2]Writing for Computer Science (4th. ed.).,Justin Zobel,2014,Springer
- [3]How to write a great research paper,Simon Peyton Jones,,Microsoft Research, Cambridge
- [4]How to Write a SIGGRAPH Paper: A Guide to Choosing a Good Research Topic, Doing the Research, and Writing It Up,David Salesin, Adobe,2012,SIGGRAPH Tutorial

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Analyze a sufficiently complex problem, generate ideas and requirements for an appropriate solution to the problem
- G2.Apply appropriate knowledge, skills, techniques and tools to design, implement and evaluate the proposed solution (either a scientific solution or a software application)
- G3.Create a technical report to communicate the proposed solution in detail
- G4.Defend the project through a poster presentation to a reviewer and the committee
- G5.Practice project management activities
- G6.Practice teamwork
• Content:
- Topic registration.
- Project proposal submission.
- Project report submission for defense.
- Project defense with the defense committee.
- Report revision and final submission after the defense.

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Introduction to the course

- Chapter 03: Access Control (VPD – MAC - OLS)

- G6.Apply security mechanisms provided by DBMSs in real-world information systems.
- G7.Illustrate  SQL Injection attacks and operate defense methods.
- G8.Explain security issues in ODBS (Outsourced Database Services) and apply security policies in an outsourced database.

# COURSE: Hệ thống thông tin phục vụ trí tuệ kinh doanh (CSC12107)

- * Data hierarchy
- Slowly changing dimension

- Chapter 7: Business intelligence mining

# COURSE: Phân tích dữ liệu ứng dụng (CSC12110)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC12110
- **English Name**: Applied Data Analytics
- **Vietnamese Name**: Phân tích dữ liệu ứng dụng
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Dr. Phạm Nguyễn Cương
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 7
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
The course helps students understand a data ecosystem and its components, as well as the fundamentals of data analysis such as data collection or data mining. Students also gain the technical and soft skills required to have effective communication of the data to stakeholders, and master these skills. These can provide students with the option to become data-driven decision-making, through exploring Big Data platforms such as Hadoop, Hive, and Spark. The course also helps students explore the fundamentals of data collection and identifying data sources, and how to clean, analyze, and share their data using visualization tools and overview pages.
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Introduce data analytics, roles and responsibilities, data analytics applications
- Introduce the data ecosystem, data structures, data formats, data storage
- Techniques related to Data Marts, Data Lakes, ETL, and Data Pipelines
- Correct exercises related to Data Marts, Data Lakes, ETL, and Data Pipelines
- Introduce big data, basic use of big data processing tools
- Experience the stages of data collection, data entry
- Technique to clean data
- Statistical analysis, data mining and tools, communication and sharing of analytical data
- Statistical analysis, data mining and tools, communication and sharing of analytical data (cont.)
- Visualizing Data and practicing some analysis tools
- Course summary/Review
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Python for Data Analysis,Wes McKinney,2018,OREILLY
- [2]Business Intelligence, Analytics, and Data Science: A Managerial Perspective,Ramesh Sharda, Dursun Delen, Efraim Turban,2019,Pearson
- [3]https://greenteapress.com/thinkstats2/thinkstats2.pdf,,,

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Use soft skills to work individual or in collaboration to effectively present technical reports according to pre-defined templates of the course
- G2.Use the key terminologies in English relating to data analysis
- G3.Explain basic concepts, responsibilities, related works, and basic ethical principles to the data analysis.
- G4.Classify the different types of data analysis and the key steps in the data analysis process, and the roles and responsibilities involved.
- G5.Demonstrate data ecosystems, the different types of data structures, data formats, data sources, and data processing languages.
- G6.Execute techniques and tools for data identification, collection, cleaning, and processing
- G7.Explain different statistical analysis tools and techniques, data visualization
• Content:
- Introduce data analytics, roles and responsibilities, data analytics applications
- Introduce the data ecosystem, data structures, data formats, data storage
- Techniques related to Data Marts, Data Lakes, ETL, and Data Pipelines
- Correct exercises related to Data Marts, Data Lakes, ETL, and Data Pipelines
- Introduce big data, basic use of big data processing tools
- Experience the stages of data collection, data entry
- Technique to clean data
- Statistical analysis, data mining and tools, communication and sharing of analytical data
- Statistical analysis, data mining and tools, communication and sharing of analytical data (cont.)
- Visualizing Data and practicing some analysis tools
- Course summary/Review

# COURSE: Quản lý dự án phần mềm (CSC13006)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC13006
- **English Name**: Software Project Management
- **Vietnamese Name**: Quản lý dự án phần mềm
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Dr. Ngô Huy Biên
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 7
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
This software project management course introduces the basic tools and techniques of software project management for students. The main goals include equipping students with the ability to plan, execute, monitor, track, and evaluate a software project.
Key topics include software development models (including a general life cycle model and specific processes such as waterfalls, RUP, Scrum, XP, Kanban with detailed activities) and project management techniques (project scope management, feasibility analysis conduction, project schedule management, project effort and cost estimation, contract negotiation, project execution, monitor, and control, team management, change management, risk management, configuration management, project quality management, project result evaluation).
This course also introduces an overview of the maturity level models and standards for improving the quality of software development process.
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Software Project, Software Project Initiation
- Software Development Models
- Work Breakdown Structure, Software Estimation
- Software Project Planning, Agile Planning
- Software Configuration Management
- Software Team Management
- Software Project Monitoring and Control
- Software Risk Management, Agile Risk Management
- Software Quality Management
- Tools for Software Project Management
- Software Process
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge,Project Management Institute,2021,Project Management Institute
- [2]An Introduction to Project Management - 6E,Kathy Schwalbe,2017,CreateSpace Independent Publishing Platform
- [3]Making Things Happen: Mastering Project Management,Scott Berkun,2008,O'Reilly Media

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Propose a software development model for a project.
- G2.Explain responsibility and ethics of a project manager when managing a software project.
- G4.Practice engineering 75,000 lines of code software with a team of 7 people.
- G3.Apply management activities to manage a software project, including determining the project scope, analyzing and selecting available products, estimating effort, time and cost of a software project, conducting feasibility analysis, creating a schedule, negotiating a contract, implementing, monitoring, and controlling a project x, leading team, change management, risk management, configuration management, project quality management, project result evaluation.
- G5.Use tools for managing software projects and software development processes.
- G6.Explain standards related to the software development process.
• Content:
- Software Project, Software Project Initiation
- Software Development Models
- Work Breakdown Structure, Software Estimation
- Software Project Planning, Agile Planning
- Software Configuration Management
- Software Team Management
- Software Project Monitoring and Control
- Software Risk Management, Agile Risk Management
- Software Quality Management
- Tools for Software Project Management
- Software Process
• Examination forms: Research Project, Oral test, Seminar
• Study and examination requirements:
- All students are responsible for reading and following strictly the regulations and policies of the school and university.

# COURSE: Các chủ đề nâng cao trong Công nghệ phần mềm (CSC13101)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC13101
- **English Name**: Advanced Topics in Software Engineering
- **Vietnamese Name**: Các chủ đề nâng cao trong Công nghệ phần mềm
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Assoc. Prof. Nguyễn Văn Vũ
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 8
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Introduction, Key concepts in software engineering
- Software engineering and Artificial Intelligence
- Software process:  traditional methods, modern methods
- Software estimation
- Software architecture: SOA, Microservices
- DevOps and CI/CD
- Software test automation
- Seminar presentations
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]The DevOps Handbook: How to Create World-Class Agility, Reliability, and Security in Technology Organizations,Gene Kim, Patrick Debois, John Willis, Jez Humble, John Allspaw,2015,IT Resolution Press
- [2]Practical DevOps - 2E,Joakim Verona,2018,Packt

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Apply teamwork skills to complete group assignments as well as to present and report seminar topics
- G2.Summarize scientific papers in the field of software engineering
- G3.Analyze and evaluate issues related to advanced topics in software engineering covered in the course
- G4.Recognize important trends having the attendion of the software engineering community
- G5.Describe and apply common approaches in software process, software estimation, software architecture, continuous integration, and test automation.
• Content:
- Introduction, Key concepts in software engineering
- Software engineering and Artificial Intelligence
- Software process:  traditional methods, modern methods
- Software estimation
- Software architecture: SOA, Microservices
- DevOps and CI/CD
- Software test automation
- Seminar presentations

- All students are responsible for reading and following strictly the regulations and policies of the school and university.

# COURSE: Kiến trúc phần mềm (CSC13106)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC13106
- **English Name**: Software Architecture
- **Vietnamese Name**: Kiến trúc phần mềm
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Trần Văn Quý
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 7
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Overview of software architecture
- Object-oriented design pattern (Creational Pattern group)
- Behavioral Patterns (group of Behavioral Patterns)
- Object-oriented design pattern (group of Structural Patterns)
- Middleware:  distributed object, methods for communicating with messages, application tier, message mapping and association, defining and implementing composite components
- Service-Oriented Architecture
- Model-Driven Architecture
- Cloud Deployment Architecture
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Software Architecture in Practice,Len Bass, Paul Clements and Rick Kazman,2021,Addison-Wesley
- [2]Clean Architecture: A Craftsman's Guide to Software Structure and Design,Robert C. Martin,2017,Pearson Professional
- [3]Fundamentals of Software Architecture: An Engineering Approach,Neal Ford, Mark Richards,2020,O'Reilly Media
- [4]Microservices for the Enterprise: Designing, Developing, and Deploying,Kasun Indrasiri, Prabath Siriwardena,2018,APRESS

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Practice actively participating in group discussions (in large groups of subjects), dividing work and coordinating work according to plans in small groups (including 2-3 students);
Identify roles and responsibilities of team members
- G2.Summarize specialized documents and lectures/slides/textbooks in English in analyzing and designing software architecture; explain some English terms in software architecture analysis and design; conduct presentation (in the form of a report written according to the prescribed form) and a presentation on the group's topic.
- G3.Practice critical thinking and holistic way of thinking when analyzing and designing software architecture
- G4.Describe the meaning of software architecture, general principles in architecture analysis and design, criteria and requirements when designing and evaluating software architecture
- G5.Conduct a presententation and analyze a number of solutions on technology and software architecture; analyze the advantages and limitations of a technology solution and software architecture
- G6.Apply some software architectures introduced in the module to software development
- G7.Propose improved solutions on available technology and software architecture for software systems
- G8.Practice personal skills such as self-study, analysis and evaluation of new technology and architecture solutions
• Content:
- Overview of software architecture
- Object-oriented design pattern (Creational Pattern group)
- Behavioral Patterns (group of Behavioral Patterns)
- Object-oriented design pattern (group of Structural Patterns)
- Middleware:  distributed object, methods for communicating with messages, application tier, message mapping and association, defining and implementing composite components
- Service-Oriented Architecture
- Model-Driven Architecture
- Cloud Deployment Architecture

# COURSE: Mô hình hóa phần mềm (CSC13108)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC13108
- **English Name**: Software Modeling
- **Vietnamese Name**: Mô hình hóa phần mềm
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Bùi Tấn Lộc
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 7
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
As software modeling plays not only an important role in the actual coding effort, we will also practice its effects in requirements engineering and testability.
Most of the course will be hands-on. Expect to write, design, discuss, and code a lot. We could apply any agile approach such as apply Scrum for our team management and use Kanban to manage our projects. We will manage our code via a git-based configuration management tool.
• COURSE TOPICS:
- An introduction to modeling and software modeling
- Business process modeling
- User requirement modeling
- Analysis modeling
- Design modeling
- Software testing specifications
- An introduction to model driven approach and model driven engineering
- Generic textual syntax modeling
- Graphical editors modeling
- Model validation and transformation
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]UML @ Classroom: An Introduction to Object-Oriented Modeling,Martina Seidl, Marion Scholz, Christian Huemer, and Gerti Kappel,2015,Springer
- [2]OOAD with UML 2 (RUP), Training materials from IBM company,,,
- [3]MDE training resources, Open Model CourseWare (OMCW) initiated by the ATLAS Team at National Institute for Research in Digital Science and Technology (INRIA),,,
- [4]UML by Example,Ghinwa Jalloul,2004,Cambridge University Press
- [5]The Business Analyst’s Handbook,Howard Podeswa,2009,Course Technology PTR
- [6]Fundamentals of Business Process Management,Marlon Dumas , Marcello La Rosa , Jan Mendling , Hajo A. Reijers,2018,Springer
- [7]Conceptual Modeling of Information Systems,Antoni Olivé,2007,Springer
- [8]Software Engineering with UML,Bhuvan Unhelkar,2017,CRC Press

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Explain necessary models used for different software development methodologies (approaches, processes, modeling languages and tools)
- G2.Apply personal skills and teamwork skills for making artifacts which are produced and used during a software development project.
- G3.Recognize how information technology enables to change business process to yield business value.
- G4.Apply, analyze, synthesize and evaluate how parts of the software development process are broken and modeled in their relations which help us facilitate agile or enterprise software development.
- G5.Synthesize test case and test plan specifications
• Content:
- An introduction to modeling and software modeling
- Business process modeling
- User requirement modeling
- Analysis modeling
- Design modeling
- Software testing specifications
- An introduction to model driven approach and model driven engineering
- Generic textual syntax modeling
- Graphical editors modeling
- Model validation and transformation
• Examination forms: Final examination, Homework, Quiz, quick test, Research Project, Mid-term examination
• Study and examination requirements:

# COURSE: Thiết kế giao diện (CSC13112)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC13112
- **English Name**: User Interface Design
- **Vietnamese Name**: Thiết kế giao diện
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Assoc. Prof. Nguyễn Văn Vũ
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 7
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
Students will have the opportunity to apply principles, methods and tools to design of the interface of software applications. Students will also discuss and evaluate user interfaces of existing applications. Through this course, students will obtain knowledge and skills in designing good user interfaces for software applications and evaluating user interface as well as practicing teamwork skills.
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Introduction
- Usability
- Learnability, Visibility, and Efficiency
- Design Process
- Task Analysis
- Prototyping
- Web UI
- Mobile UI
- Graphic Design
- Interaction Styles
- Evaluating Interface Designs
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Don't Make Me Think, Revisited: A Common Sense Approach to Web Usability,Steve Krug,2014,Pearson Education
- [2]Sketching User Experiences: Getting the Design Right and the Right Design,William Buxton,2007,Morgan Kaufmann

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Pratice working in teams to design the user interface of a software application and report results
- G2.Summarize papers and materials in English related to user interface design
- G3.Analyze and evaluate user interfaces of existing applications
- G4.Explain the importance, basic concepts, and components of user interface
- G5.Explain and apply basic principles of user interface for designing desktop, Web, and mobile applications
- G6.Explain and apply processes, methods, and tools for designing user interfaces effectively
- G6.Practice implementing the user interface of software applications according the design
- G7.Apply programming skills to implement user interfaces of the proposed application
• Content:
- Introduction
- Usability
- Learnability, Visibility, and Efficiency
- Design Process
- Task Analysis
- Prototyping
- Web UI
- Mobile UI
- Graphic Design
- Interaction Styles
- Evaluating Interface Designs
• Examination forms: Final examination, In-class Activities, Quiz, quick test, Research Project, Individual Presentation

# COURSE: Các công nghệ mới trong phát triển phần mềm (CSC13115)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC13115
- **English Name**: Modern Software Development Technologies
- **Vietnamese Name**: Các công nghệ mới trong phát triển phần mềm
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Trần Văn Quý
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 8
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
This course introduces new technology trends in software development, providing students with foundational knowledge and experiences in building and expanding software by applying modern technologies.
Core topics of the course include building blockchain systems, building chatbots by applying AI services, handling real-time events, installing and configuring cloud infrastructure systems cloud, and interacting with the system through augmented reality.
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Introduction & overview of new technologies in software development
- Blockchain overview
- Blockchain design and implementation: Blocks, Chain, Blockchain Network, Proof of Work
- Wallets and Transactions, Transaction Pool, Mine Transactions
- Other topics: Smart Contracts, Ethereum, Actual use cases using Blockchain
- Cloud Services (Amazon / Azure / Google Cloud): Analyze photos and videos, Text To Speech, Speech To Text, Translation
- + Analyze photos and videos
- Using machine learning libraries on mobile and web platforms
- Using artificial intelligence services
- New mobile programming techniques: ML, Vision, IoT
- AR Technology
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Mastering Blockchain: Unlocking the Power of Cryptocurrencies, Smart Contracts, and Decentralized Applications,Lorne Lantz, Daniel Cawrey,2020,O'Reilly Media
- [2]Applied Deep Learning with Python: Use scikit-learn, TensorFlow, and Keras to create intelligent systems and machine learning solutions,Alex Galea , Luis Capelo,2018,Packt Publishing

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Practice working on an individual level and collaborate as a team to implement projects that apply new technologies in software development.
- G2.Explain English terms about new technology trends in software development.
- G3.Explain basic concepts, terms, and use cases of new technology trends in software development.
- G4.Apply new technologies to develop a software product.
- G5.Use software tools to develop applications that use new technologies.
• Content:
- Introduction & overview of new technologies in software development
- Blockchain overview
- Blockchain design and implementation: Blocks, Chain, Blockchain Network, Proof of Work
- Wallets and Transactions, Transaction Pool, Mine Transactions
- Other topics: Smart Contracts, Ethereum, Actual use cases using Blockchain
- Cloud Services (Amazon / Azure / Google Cloud): Analyze photos and videos, Text To Speech, Speech To Text, Translation
- + Analyze photos and videos
- Using machine learning libraries on mobile and web platforms
- Using artificial intelligence services
- New mobile programming techniques: ML, Vision, IoT
- AR Technology
• Examination forms: Final examination, Homework, Research Project

# COURSE: Automata và ngôn ngữ hình thức (CSC14001)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC14001
- **English Name**: Automata and Formal Languages
- **Vietnamese Name**: Automata và ngôn ngữ hình thức
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Dr. Nguyễn Thanh Phương
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 6
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Introduction to the basic mathematics for automata theory and formal languages
- Introduction to automata and formal languages
- Finite automata
- Regular expressions
- Regular grammars and regular languages
- Context-free grammars and context-free languages
- Push-down automata
- Turing machines and Church-Turing thesis
- The Chomsky Hierarchy and Beyond
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]The Theory of Formal Languages,Phuong N.T.,2018,Science and Technics Publishing House
- [2]Introduction to Automata Theory, Languages, and Computation, 3rd Edition,J. E. Hopcroft, R. Motwani, J. D. Ullman,2006,Prentice Hall
- [3]Introduction to the Theory of Computation, 3rd Edition,M. Sipser,2012,Cengage Learning
- [4]Automata, Computability and Complexity: Theory and Applications,E. A. Rich,2008,Prentice Hall

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Analyze and propose reasonable solutions to problems in computer science.
- G2.Interpret English terminology in the theory of computer science.
- G3.Explain the nature, role, and operation of computational structures and their variants.
- G4.Analyze the properties of classes of formal languages.
- G5.Construct automata recognizing each class of formal languages.
- G6.Construct formal grammars generating each class of formal languages.
• Content:
- Introduction to the basic mathematics for automata theory and formal languages
- Introduction to automata and formal languages
- Finite automata
- Regular expressions

# COURSE: Các hệ cơ sở tri thức (CSC14002)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC14002
- **English Name**: Knowledge-Based Systems
- **Vietnamese Name**: Các hệ cơ sở tri thức
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Assoc. Prof. Lê Hoàng Thái
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 8
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Class overview: Class organization, topics overview, software etc.
- A Guide to Agency-Wide Knowledge Management for State DOTs
- Knowledge Processes & Knowledge Work Systems
- Foundations of  Artificial Intelligence
- Neural Networks
- Neural Networks (continue)
- Genetic Algorithms
- Genetic Algorithms(continue)
- FUZZY LOGIC
- Fuzzy Logic Control
- Presentations
- Review
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Knowledge Based System A Complete Guide,Gerardus Blokdyk,2020,5STARCooks
- [2]Knowledge-Based Systems,Rajendra Akerkar, Priti Sajja,2009,Jones & Bartlett Learning

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Discuss about Knowledge management and its broad applications in various aspects of our day-to-day life.
- G2.Operate the algorithms used in various phases of Knowledge-based systems, including Knowledge management process and Knowledge Works Systems (KWS).
- G3.Employ the techniques used in Knowledge-based systems, such as representation of knowledge (knowledge base), reasoning processes (inference/reasoning), neural networks, Fuzzy logic, genetic algorithms, etc.
- G4.Summarize knowledge of various applications of Knowledge-based systems in real life, including reading research papers and preparing presentations.
- G5.Produce programs for Knowledge-based systems and implement the various techniques discussed throughout the course.
• Content:
- Class overview: Class organization, topics overview, software etc.

- Knowledge Processes & Knowledge Work Systems
- Foundations of  Artificial Intelligence
- Neural Networks

# COURSE: Cơ sở trí tuệ nhân tạo (CSC14003)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC14003
- **English Name**: Fundamentals of Artificial Intelligence
- **Vietnamese Name**: Cơ sở trí tuệ nhân tạo
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Dr. Nguyễn Ngọc Thảo
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 5
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Introduction to AI
- Intelligent Agents
- Solving Problem by Searching: Basic concepts and Uninformed search
- Solving Problem by Searching: Informed search
- Local Search
- Adversarial Search
- Constraint Satisfaction Problem
- Midterm Examination
- Propositional Logic: Basic concepts
- Propositional Logic: Inference Mechanisms
- First-Order Logic: Syntax, Semantics, and Inference Mechanisms
- Machine learning: Basic concepts and ID3 Decision tree
- Perceptron and Multi-layer neural networks
- Reviews
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach,Stuart Russell, Peter Norvig,2020,Pearson

- G4.Develop simple learning agents that learn by observations.
- G5.Practice generating hypotheses and assess them through theoretical and/or empirical evaluations.
- G6.Recognize textbook, assignments and practice writing reports in English.
- G7.Organize tasks while conducting individual and group work.

# COURSE: Nhập môn học máy (CSC14005)

# COURSE: Ẩn dữ liệu và chia sẻ thông tin (CSC14101)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC14101
- **English Name**: Data Hiding and Steganography
- **Vietnamese Name**: Ẩn dữ liệu và chia sẻ thông tin
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Phạm Trọng Nghĩa
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 8
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Data Hiding Overview
- The Problem of Hiding
- Data on Text
- The problem of Hiding
- Data on images
- The Problem of Hiding
- Data on Audio
- Sharing confidential information
- Other Data Hiding
- Techniques
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Data Hiding and Its Applications: Digital Watermarking and Steganography,David Meg´ıas, Wojciech Mazurczyk, Minoru Kuribayashi,2022,Mdpi AG

- G1.Explain English terms, writing, and presentation skills related to the field of Data Hiding and Secret Sharing.
- G2.Apply knowledge of math, physics, and programming.
- G3.Explain basic concepts, terms, and issues related to data hiding, including watermarking (digital watermarking), steganography (secret communication), applications of the field, and models for sharing confidential information.
- G4.Apply appropriate data hiding techniques to given requirements of real problems.
- G5.Construct the appropriate data sharing and security models based on the structure of each type of information and the unique requirements of each system
- G6.Operate the copyright of intellectual products.
- G7.Analyze and/or aggregate documents and write scientific reports.

- The Problem of Hiding
- Data on Text
- The problem of Hiding

# COURSE: Trình biên dịch (CSC14113)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC14113
- **English Name**: Compiler
- **Vietnamese Name**: Trình biên dịch
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Dr. Nguyễn Thanh Phương
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 8
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
This course will discuss the major ideas used in the implementation of compilers, including lexical analysis, syntax analysis, semantic analysis, syntax-directed translation, abstract syntax trees, symbol table generation, intermediate code and intermediate code generation, code generation, and runtime systems. As part of the course, students will write a small compiler for a C-like language.
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Introduction to compiler design
- An overview of a simple compiler
- Lexical analysis
- Syntax analysis
- Symbol table generation
- Semantic analysis
- Intermediate code representation
- Intermediate code generation
- Runtime environment
- Target code generation
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Compilers: Principles, Techniques, and Tools,A. Aho, M. Lam, R. Sethi, J. Ullman,2006,Addison Wesley

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Discuss the role and organization of a programming language implementation system.
- G2.Interpret English terminology in the field of compiler design and construction.
- G3.Explain the role of Runtime environment.
- G4.Explain the role, design, and operation of the Front-end phase.
- G5.Explain the role, design, and operation of the Back-end phase.
• Content:
- Introduction to compiler design
- An overview of a simple compiler
- Lexical analysis
- Syntax analysis
- Symbol table generation
- Semantic analysis
- Intermediate code representation
- Intermediate code generation
- Runtime environment
- Target code generation

# COURSE: Nhập môn dữ liệu lớn (CSC14118)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC14118
- **English Name**: Introduction to Big Data
- **Vietnamese Name**: Nhập môn dữ liệu lớn
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Dr. Nguyễn Ngọc Thảo
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 6
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
Students are provided an overview of the actively growing field of Big Data, its key issues in data management and associated applications in intelligent business and scientific studies. Their practical experiences will be strengthened through the manipulation of the tools for managing and analyzing Big Data like Hadoop MapReduce for batch processing, Spark for streaming processing and NoSQL databases. Those enables students to build their own skills to solve complex real-world problems in for decision support.
• COURSE TOPICS:
- The Fundamentals of Big Data
- Hadoop Fundamentals: Basic concepts and Ecosystem
- Hadoop Distributed Filesystem
- Hadoop MapReduce
- Spark Fundamentals: Basic concepts and Ecosystem
- Spark APIs
- Advanced Analytics with Spark
- Midterm examination
- NoSQL databases: MongoDB
- Stream processing: Spark Streaming
- Seminar
- Course review
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Hadoop: The Definitive Guide (4th edition),Tom White,2015,O'Reilly Media
- [2]Learning Spark: Lightning-Fast Data Analytics 2nd Edition,Jules Damji, Brooke Wenig, Tathagata Das, Denny Lee,2020,O'Reilly Media
- [3]Spark: The Definitive Guide: Big Data Processing Made Simple,Bill Chambers and Matei Zaharia,2018,O'Reilly Media

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Discuss the key principles of Big Data and motivation for moving from classical data analysis to more advanced forms.
- G2.Acquire fundamental enabling techniques and scalable algorithms like Hadoop Map Reduce, Spark and NoSQL for large-scale data analytics.
- G3.Describe adequate perspectives of Big Data analytics by introducing various tools for large-scale data analytics and applying Big Data in various applications.
- G4.Select and implement machine learning/data analytics techniques and computing environments suitable for the applications under consideration.
- G5.Investigate designated online courses about some Big Data topics and complete most learning items in that course.
• Content:
- The Fundamentals of Big Data
- Hadoop Fundamentals: Basic concepts and Ecosystem
- Hadoop Distributed Filesystem
- Hadoop MapReduce
- Spark Fundamentals: Basic concepts and Ecosystem
- Spark APIs
- Advanced Analytics with Spark
- Midterm examination
- NoSQL databases: MongoDB
- Stream processing: Spark Streaming
- Seminar
- Course review
• Examination forms: Final examination, Written Report, Group Presentation, Quiz, quick test, Mid-term examination

- All students are responsible for reading and following strictly the regulations and policies of the school and university.

# COURSE: Lập trình song song (CSC14120)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC14120
- **English Name**: Parallel Programming
- **Vietnamese Name**: Lập trình song song
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Trần Trung Kiên
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 7
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Introduction to CUDA; example: vector addition, matrix multiplication, convolution, … (2 weeks)
- GPU parallel execution in CUDA; example: reduction, vector addition, … (3 weeks)
- Types of GPU memories in CUDA; example: reduction, convolution, … (2 weeks)
- Example: scan, histogram, sort (3 weeks)
- Optimizing a CUDA program; additional topics in parallel programming; final project (1 weeks)
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Programming in Parallel with CUDA: A Practical Guide,Richard Ansorge,2022,Cambridge University Press
- [2]Nhập môn CUDA,Lê Hoài Bắc, Phạm Hoài Vũ,2012,ĐHQG HCM
- [3]Lập Trình Song Song Trên GPU,Lê Hoài Bắc, Vũ Thanh Hưng, Trần Trung Kiên,2015,Nhà xuất bản Khoa học và kỹ thuật

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Operate common tasks (vector addition, matrix multiplication, convolution, reduction, scan, histogram, sort, ...) for parallel execution on the GPU using CUDA.
- G2.Apply the knowledge of GPU parallel execution in CUDA to speed up a CUDA program.
- G3.Apply the knowledge of GPU memories in CUDA to speed up a CUDA program.
- G4.Apply the optimization process to optimize a CUDA program.
- G5.Apply teamwork skills to complete the final project.
• Content:
- Introduction to CUDA; example: vector addition, matrix multiplication, convolution, … (2 weeks)
- GPU parallel execution in CUDA; example: reduction, vector addition, … (3 weeks)
- Types of GPU memories in CUDA; example: reduction, convolution, … (2 weeks)
- Example: scan, histogram, sort (3 weeks)
- Optimizing a CUDA program; additional topics in parallel programming; final project (1 weeks)

# COURSE: Mã hóa ứng dụng (CSC15003)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC15003
- **English Name**: Applied Cryptography
- **Vietnamese Name**: Mã hóa ứng dụng
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Trần Anh Duy
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 6
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Introduction to Security Requirements, Services and Mechanisms of Security.
- Introduction to Cryptography:
- Classical Cipher
- Classification
- Security Evaluation
- Perfect Secrecy.
- Symmetric Cryptography:
- Block Ciphers
- Feistel Cipher
- DES
- Number Theory Refresher
- IDEA, AES
- Cascade of ciphers
- Stream Ciphers, RC4
- Asymmetric Cryptography:
- Number theory
- One-way functions
- Diffie-Hellman
- RSA
- El Gamal
- Elliptic Curve Cryptography
- Data Encryption Mechanism:
- Statistical Attacks
- Chaining Modes (CBC, CFB, OFB, CTR, XTS)
- Hash Functions and Integrity
- Hash Functions
- MAC, MDC
- Security Properties
- Alternatives for MAC
- Digital Signatures and Non-Repudiation
- El Gamal Signature Algorithm
- Digital Signature Standard
- Non-repudiation of receipt
- Authentication
- Classification
- Authentication Protocols
- Passwords
- Smartcards
- Key Management
- Key Generation
- Symmetric Key Distribution
- Kerberos
- Public-key Certification and PKI systems
- Law Enforcement
- Optional topics:
- Security for Database.
- Authorization/Access Control
- Advanced topics:
- Searchable Encryption.
- Blockchain.
- Quantum Cryptography
- Securing Microservice with mTLS, JWT and gRPC.
- Applied Cryptography.
- Review
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Cryptography Theory and Practice 4th Edition,Douglas Robert Stinson,2017,Maura Paterson

- [5]Quantum Cryptography: From Key Distribution to Conference Key Agreement,Federico Grasselli,2021,Springer

- G1.Apply team work and individual work skills to learn the basic concepts of applied cryptography
- G2.Know and explain English terminology in the field of Cryptography
- G3.Understand the basic concepts, terms, responsibility and fundamental principles in the field of Cryptography
- G4.Identify and categorize Cryptography algorithms for real world application.
- G5.Understand and apply techniques in the field of applying Cryptography.
- G6.Understand the methods to attack Cryptography algorithms.

# COURSE: Nhập môn xử lý ngôn ngữ tự nhiên (CSC15006)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC15006
- **English Name**: Introduction to Natural Language Processing
- **Vietnamese Name**: Nhập môn xử lý ngôn ngữ tự nhiên
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Assoc. Prof. Đinh Điền - Nguyễn Hồng Bửu Long
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 5
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Introduction to Natural Languages
- An Introduction to Formal Languages: Representation and Grammar of Language
- An Introduction to Formal Languages: Production rules, Derivation trees and syntactic parsing
- Rule-based  approaches: Earley algorithm
- Corpus-based approaches: TBL and POS tagging
- Language models in the spelling correction task
- Hybrid approaches
- Pre-processing in NLP
- Understanding text classification problems based on statistical probability methods
- Apply to the sentiment analysis task
- Morphological analysis
- Syntactic analysis
- Syntactic parsing
- Other NLP applications
- Review
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Xử lý Ngôn ngữ Tự nhiên,Đinh Điền,2006,NXB ĐHQG

- [3]Natural Language Processing with PyTorch,Delip Rao and Brian McMahan,2019,O’Reilly Media

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Understand the basic concepts of natural language processing (NLP), terminologies in natural language, the similarities and differences between English and Vietnamese in terms of automatic language processing.
- G2.Understand basic NLP problems such as morphological analysis, grammar analysis.
- G3.Apply parsing methods according to rule-based approaches and machine learning based approaches.
- G4.Utilize existing NLP toolkits to process texts
- G5.Understand and utilize existing processing blocks in NLP systems
- G6.Apply individual and group working skills to research and solve NLP problems.
- G7.Search and read technical documents in English.

- Introduction to Natural Languages
- An Introduction to Formal Languages: Representation and Grammar of Language
- An Introduction to Formal Languages: Production rules, Derivation trees and syntactic parsing
- Rule-based  approaches: Earley algorithm
- Corpus-based approaches: TBL and POS tagging
- Language models in the spelling correction task
- Hybrid approaches

# COURSE: Xử lý tín hiệu số (CSC15009)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC15009
- **English Name**: Digital Signal Processing
- **Vietnamese Name**: Xử lý tín hiệu số
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Thái Hùng Văn
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 7
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Overview of signals & digital signal processing.
- Digital signal sampling, playback and some basic processing
- Discrete signal processing systems and linear invariant response
- Basic transform on discrete signals - Z transform
- Fourier, Waveless, LPC transforms
- Review - Relationship between processes and transformations.
- Noise Filter
- Embedding & hiding data in digital signals
- Compress & decompress digital signals
- Digital voice and audio signal processing
- Review
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Discrete-Time Signal Processing,Alan V. Oppenheim and Ronald W. Schafer,2009,Pearson

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Understand the specialized English term and materials related to data recovery and safety (DSR)
- G2.Understand the basic knowledge and theory in Digital Signal
Processing
- G3.Understand basic concepts, properties of discrete time systems, analyze & calculate impulse response and response of invariant linear system
- G4.Understand and apply the fundamental transformations Z, Fourier
- G5.Understand and apply some methods of noise filtering, embedding or hiding data & signal compression
- G6.Understand and apply some basic processing with digital audio signals.
• Content:
- Overview of signals & digital signal processing.
- Digital signal sampling, playback and some basic processing
- Discrete signal processing systems and linear invariant response
- Basic transform on discrete signals - Z transform
- Fourier, Waveless, LPC transforms
- Review - Relationship between processes and transformations.
- Noise Filter
- Embedding & hiding data in digital signals
- Compress & decompress digital signals
- Digital voice and audio signal processing
- Review

# COURSE: Nhập môn ngôn ngữ học thống kê và ứng dụng (CSC15011)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC15011
- **English Name**: Introduction to Statistics Linguistics and Applications
- **Vietnamese Name**: Nhập môn ngôn ngữ học thống kê và ứng dụng
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Assoc. Prof. Đinh Điền - Nguyễn Hồng Bửu Long
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 7
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- An overview of statistical linguistics:
- Probability theory
- Random variables, probability distribution
- Statistical linguistics
- Parameter estimation and statistical hypothesis testing
- Correlation and regression
- Frequency dictionary
- Quantitative learning style
- Quantitative organization of vocabulary
- Diversity of vocabulary
- Introduction to linguistics:
- Introduction
- History of corpus research
- Linguistic theory
- Building a corpus:
- Planning to build a corpus
- Criteria for selecting a corpus
- Types of a corpus
- Building a corpus (continued):
- Collect data
- Building a monolingual corpus (Vietnamese)
- Building a bilingual corpus (English-Vietnamese)
- Processing a corpus:
- Pre-processing of a corpus
- Language labeling
- Formalization of language labels
- Processing the corpus (continued):
- Language labeling for a monolingual corpus (Vietnamese)
- Language labeling for a bilingual corpus (English-Vietnamese)
- Designing a bilingual corpus (English-Vietnamese)
- Processing a corpus (continued):
- Corpus marking
- Metadata
- Spoken text corpus
- Applications:
- In computational linguistics
- In language research
- In language teaching
- In other language-related applications
- Seminar
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Ngôn ngữ học Ngữ liệu,Đinh Điền,2018,NXB ĐHQG-HCM
- [2]Corpus Linguistics and Linguistically Annotated Corpora,Sandra Kuebler, Heike Zinsmeister,2015,Bloomsbury Academic

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Apply team work and individual work skills to research, construct, and exploit corpora
- G2.Understand and apply at a basic level statistical methods, evaluating on real data
- G3.Know the basic concepts of corpus, classification of corpus; criteria for building corpus, processing and exploiting corpus
- G4.Identify, describe, analyze, and evaluate the structure and quality of a particular corpus
- G6.Recognize the role and importance of statistical linguistics in preparing corpora
- G5.Know and apply the criteria when collecting corpus (corpus balancing, representativeness, corpus sampling); corpus processing: language labeling at different linguistic levels and at different language levels; corpus mining
• Content:
- An overview of statistical linguistics:
- Probability theory
- Random variables, probability distribution
- Statistical linguistics
- Parameter estimation and statistical hypothesis testing
- Correlation and regression
- Frequency dictionary
- Quantitative learning style
- Quantitative organization of vocabulary
- Diversity of vocabulary
- Introduction to linguistics:
- Introduction
- History of corpus research
- Linguistic theory
- Building a corpus:
- Planning to build a corpus
- Criteria for selecting a corpus
- Types of a corpus
- Building a corpus (continued):
- Collect data
- Building a monolingual corpus (Vietnamese)
- Building a bilingual corpus (English-Vietnamese)
- Processing a corpus:
- Pre-processing of a corpus
- Language labeling
- Formalization of language labels
- Processing the corpus (continued):
- Language labeling for a monolingual corpus (Vietnamese)
- Language labeling for a bilingual corpus (English-Vietnamese)
- Designing a bilingual corpus (English-Vietnamese)
- Processing a corpus (continued):
- Corpus marking
- Metadata
- Spoken text corpus
- Applications:
- In computational linguistics
- In language research
- In language teaching
- In other language-related applications
- Seminar

# COURSE: Phân tích dữ liệu bảo toàn tính riêng tư (CSC15107)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC15107
- **English Name**: Privacy-Preserving Data Analysis

- Application: DP and Neural Network and Deep Learning.

- G6.Use supporting software tools.

# COURSE: Phân tích thống kê dữ liệu nhiều biến (CSC16003)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC16003
- **English Name**: Multivariate Statistical Analysis
- **Vietnamese Name**: Phân tích thống kê dữ liệu nhiều biến
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Assoc. Prof. Lý Quốc Ngọc
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 6

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Introduction to MSA
- The fundamental concepts of MSA (sample)
- The fundamental concepts of MSA (populations) (cont.)
- Multivariate Linear Regression (sample)
- Multivariate Linear Regression.(population)
- Principal Component Analysis (sample)
- Principal Component Analysis (population) (cont.)
- Factor Analysis (sample)
- Factor Analysis (population)
- Canonical Correlation Analysis
- Multivariate Clustering
- Multivariate Classification
- Seminar on subject project 1 (Topic: Basic concepts in MSA support statistical analysis of university exam scores.)

# COURSE: Xử lý ảnh số và video số (CSC16005)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC16005
- **English Name**: Digital Image and Video Processing
- **Vietnamese Name**: Xử lý ảnh số và video số
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Assoc. Prof. Lý Quốc Ngọc
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 5
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
This course aims to provide students the fundamental knowledge and advanced knowledge in digital image and video processing (image|video representation, image|video preprocessing, image|video segmentation, image transformation, image compression). The course supports students accumulate skills in building basic image and video processing systems for practical applications. Based on the provided knowledge and skills, students will easily acquire knowledge from subjects of  Visual Data Science.
And more importantly, the subject helps the students understand the principles of solutions and acghieve experience through practical experience in building specific applications that meet the requirements of Industrial Revolution 4.0.
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Introduction to DIP
- The fundamental concepts of DIP
- Image Color Transformations
- Geometric Transformations
- Image Smoothing on Spatial Domain.
- Edge Detection on Spatial Domain.
- Image Segmentation
- Video Segmentation
- Discrete Fourier Transform and Applications
- Karhunen-Loève Transformation
- Image Compression
- Seminar on subject project 1 (Topic: Semantic Image Segmentation)
- Seminar on subject project 2 (Topic: Image Restoration)
- Seminar on subject project 3 (Image Forgeries Detection, Watermarking-Steganography)
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Digital Image Processing 4th Edition,Rafael C. Gonzalez, Richard E. Woods,2017,Pearson
- [2]Learning Image Processing with OpenCV,Gloria Bueno García Oscar Deniz Suarez José Luis Espinosa Aranda Jesus Salido Tercero Ismael Serrano Gracia Noelia Vállez Enano,2015,Packt Publishing

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Recognize the roles of individuals and groups in group activities in response to learning about digital image and video processing (DIP).
- G2.Recognize English documents and Presentation in English about DIP.
- G3.Recognize analytical and problem-solving methods related to the DIP.
- G4.Interpret and apply process of designing and implementing image|video pre-processing.
- G5.Interpret and apply process of designing and implementing image|video transformations.
- G6.Interpret and apply process of designing and implementingimage|video segmentation.
- G7.Interpret and apply process of designing and implementingimage|video compression.
- G8.Practice a simple process of designing and implementing an image|video processing system.
• Content:
- Introduction to DIP
- The fundamental concepts of DIP
- Image Color Transformations
- Geometric Transformations
- Image Smoothing on Spatial Domain.
- Edge Detection on Spatial Domain.
- Image Segmentation
- Video Segmentation
- Discrete Fourier Transform and Applications
- Karhunen-Loève Transformation
- Image Compression
- Seminar on subject project 1 (Topic: Semantic Image Segmentation)
- Seminar on subject project 2 (Topic: Image Restoration)
- Seminar on subject project 3 (Image Forgeries Detection, Watermarking-Steganography)

# COURSE: Ứng dụng xử lý ảnh số và video số (CSC16109)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC16109
- **English Name**: Applications in Digital Image and Video Processing
- **Vietnamese Name**: Ứng dụng xử lý ảnh số và video số
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Assoc. Prof. Lý Quốc Ngọc
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 7
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
This course is an urgent bridge from basic knowledge in Digital Image and Video Processing, Computer Vision and Computer Graphics to practical applications that society needs, in line with the training process according to the 4th technology revolution.
This course is intended to provide students with practical experience in designing the applications related to image & video. The content includes the main parts such as applications in the fields of digital marketing and e-commerce; smart city construction;  self-driving devices such as Mobile Robot and Drone devices; 3D object reconstruction for 3D printing technology; Human-machine interaction; Medical image processing; Intelligent Agriculture.
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Introduction  to Applied  DIP
- The role of DIP in digital marketing and e-commerce
- Product sample Recognition, Retrieval
- The role of DIP in smart city
- Object Retrieval VQA on security monitoring
- Abnormal Event Detection VQA on security monitoring
- Role of DIP in Self-Driving Devices
- Video Stabilization on Mobile Robot, Drone
- Locolization and 3D Scene Reconstruction on Mobile robot, Drone
- Role of 3D Object Reconstruction
- 3D Object Reconstruction based on Depth-Color Images Sequence
- Role of  DIP in Human-Machine Interaction
- Face Detection & Recognition based on Facial Attributes
- Human Activity Recognition
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Digital Image Processing 4th Edition,Rafael C. gonzalez, Richard E. Woods,2019,Springer
- [2]Learning Image Processing with OpenCV,Gloria Bueno García Oscar Deniz Suarez José Luis Espinosa Aranda Jesus Salido Tercero Ismael Serrano Gracia Noelia Vállez Enano, Packt Publishing,2015,Packt Publishing

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Practice the roles of individuals and groups in group activities in response to learning about applied digital image and video processing (Applied DIP).
- G2.Practice English documents and Presentation in English about Applied   DIP.
- G3.Analyze analytical and problem-solving methods related to the Applied DIP.
- G4.Analyze process of designing and implementing application in digital marketing and e-commerce..
- G5.Analyze process of designing and implementing application  in smart city construction.
- G6.Analyze process of designing and implementing application  in self-driving devices.
- G7.Analyze process of designing and implementing application  in 3D printing technology.
- G8.Analyze a simple process of designing and implementing application  in human-machine interaction.
• Content:
- Introduction  to Applied  DIP
- The role of DIP in digital marketing and e-commerce
- Product sample Recognition, Retrieval
- The role of DIP in smart city
- Object Retrieval VQA on security monitoring
- Abnormal Event Detection VQA on security monitoring
- Role of DIP in Self-Driving Devices
- Video Stabilization on Mobile Robot, Drone
- Locolization and 3D Scene Reconstruction on Mobile robot, Drone
- Role of 3D Object Reconstruction
- 3D Object Reconstruction based on Depth-Color Images Sequence
- Role of  DIP in Human-Machine Interaction
- Face Detection & Recognition based on Facial Attributes
- Human Activity Recognition
• Examination forms: Final examination, Lab mid-term exam, Lab final exam, Homework, Research Project, Case Study, Essay, Excercise, Mid-term examination

# COURSE: Khai thác dữ liệu đồ thị (CSC17103)

## GENERAL INFORMATION

- **English Name**: Graph Mining
- **Vietnamese Name**: Khai thác dữ liệu đồ thị
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Lê Ngọc Thành
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 8
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Introducing Graph Data Mining
- Review knowledge related to graphs and data mining
- Samples in static and dynamic graphs; generate graph.
- Graph indexing and ranking
- Mining graph patterns
- Graph Classification
- Clustering and community discovery
- Link Prediction
- Embedding
- Research topics include deep learning for graphs, graph abstracts, recommendation systems, anomaly detection, large-sized graphs.
- Research topics include deep learning for graphs, graph abstracts, recommendation systems, anomaly detection, large-sized graphs. (cont)

- G4.Describe the meaning and application of graph data mining problems to real-world problems.
- G5.Explain how basic algorithms work in graph data mining.
- G6.Construct some components of a small-scale graph data mining system.
- G7.Operate tools and libraries.

# COURSE: Lập trình cho khoa học dữ liệu (CSC17104)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: CSC17104
- **English Name**: Programming for Data Science
- **Vietnamese Name**: Lập trình cho khoa học dữ liệu
- **Responsible Person**: for the module: Trần Trung Kiên

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:

- Linux commands, Conda
- Git & Github
- Jupyter Notebook, Markdown
- Python
- Numpy
- Pandas, Matplotlib
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:

- G7.Use Git, Github, and teamwork skills to control versions and collaborate with others.

- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 25 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 116.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
This course will allow you to learn in a very simple way how to identify, design and develop analytical information systems, such as Business Intelligence with a descriptive analysis on data warehouses. A data warehouse is a large repository of data that has been cleaned to a consistent quality. Not all data repositories are used in the same way or require the same rigor when choosing what data to store. Data warehouses are designed to enable rapid business decision making through accurate and flexible reporting and data analysis. A data warehouse is one of the most fundamental business intelligence tools in use today, and one that successful Data Engineers must understand. This course also describes different kinds of repositories including data marts, data lakes, and data reservoirs and explains their functions and uses. Students will be introduced to the problem of integration and predictive analysis of high volume of unstructured data and the definition of the Extract, Transform, Load (ETL) process as well as touch on data warehousing in the big data framework. Students will be provided insight into methods and tools for analysis and processing of the data generated by modern information systems. Besides, real-time data analytics looks at a portion of big data that deals with streaming data will be introduced in this course.
• COURSE TOPICS:
- An Overview of Business Intelligence, Analytics, and Data Science
- Nature of Data and Visualization
- An Introduction to Data Warehouses, Data Marts, and Data Lakes
- Designing, Modeling and Implementing Data Warehouses
- NoSQL engines, stream systems
- Introducing Real-Time Analytics
- Data Processing Techniques
- ETL & Data Pipelines: Tools and Techniques
- Building Data Pipelines: Tools and Techniques
- Building Streaming Pipelines: Tools and Techniques
- Review
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Business Intelligence, Analytics, and Data Science,Ramesh Sharda, Dursun Delen, Efraim Turban,2019,Pearson
- [2]The Enterprise Big Data Lake Delivering the Promise of Big Data and Data Science (1st Edition),Alex Gorelik,2019,O'Reilly Media

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Practice teamwork skills
- G2.Practice reading technique documents in English
- G3.Practice critical and creative thinking in study process
- G4.Explain Business Intelligence, Analytics, and Data Science
- G5.Express the different kinds of repositories including data ware house, data marts, data lakes, and data reservoirs, and explain their functions and uses.
- G6.Experiment the additional technologies to bring business intelligence to the big data era.
• Content:
- An Overview of Business Intelligence, Analytics, and Data Science
- Nature of Data and Visualization
- An Introduction to Data Warehouses, Data Marts, and Data Lakes
- Designing, Modeling and Implementing Data Warehouses
- NoSQL engines, stream systems
- Introducing Real-Time Analytics
- Data Processing Techniques
- ETL & Data Pipelines: Tools and Techniques
- Building Data Pipelines: Tools and Techniques
- Building Streaming Pipelines: Tools and Techniques
- Review
• Examination forms: Close Test, Final examination, Written Report, Lab Report, Homework, Group Presentation, Quiz, quick test, Individual Presentation, Case Study

# COURSE: Khoa học Trái đất (GEO00002)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: GEO00002
- **English Name**: Earth Science
- **Vietnamese Name**: Khoa học Trái đất
- **Responsible Person**: for the module:

- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 25 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 0 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 57.5 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Concepts and Foundations of Environmental Science
- Earth in Space (Solar System, Universe, Life Cycle of Stars)
- Physical and Chemical Properties, Composition, Origin, and Age of Earth
- Composition and Structure of the Atmosphere; Water in the Atmosphere
- Wind and Weather
- Energy in the Atmosphere; Climate Change and Air Pollution
- Freshwater on Earth
- Seas and Oceans
- Surface Materials of the Earth: Minerals, Soil, and Rocks – Weathering, Erosion, and Accumulation (Gravitational Activities)
- Earthquakes, Volcanoes, and Plate Tectonics
- History of the Earth
- Minerals and Energy on Earth
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Earth Science,DANIELSON, E.W., DENECKE, E.J.Jr.,1986,

- G4.Understand the history of the Earth through records of fossils and stratigraphy
- G5.Apply this knowledge to explain certain issues in the main field of study
- G6.Have skills in group discussion, presentation, and critical thinking
- G7.Attitude: enthusiastic, honest in learning; serious and truthful in examinations

# COURSE: Sinh đại cương 1 (BIO00001)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: BIO00001
- **English Name**: Biology 1
- **Vietnamese Name**: Sinh đại cương 1
- **Responsible Person**: for the module:
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 2
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 0 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 86.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Introduction
- Basic concepts of biology.
- The Chemical Basis of Life
- Matter and chemical reactions.
- Matter and chemical reactions (continued).
- Water and life.
- Water and life (continued).
- Carbon skeletons and biological molecules.
- An overview of cell structure and function.
- The exchange of matter across cell membranes.
- Cellular metabolism processes: cellular respiration and fermentation.
- Cellular metabolism processes: cellular respiration and fermentation (continued).
- Cellular metabolism processes: photosynthesis.
- Structure of DNA, mechanisms of replication and DNA repair.
- Structure of chromosomes - Chromosome variations.
- Cell cycle, mitosis, and meiosis.
- Bacterial and viral genetics.
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]SINH HỌC ĐẠI CƯƠNG: Tế bào học, Di truyền học và Học thuyết Tiến hóa,Phạm Thành Hổ,2011,Nhà Xuất Bản Đại học Quốc Gia TP. Hồ Chí Minh
- [2]Sinh học tế bào,Bùi Trang Việt,2012,Nhà Xuất Bản Đại học Quốc Gia TP. Hồ Chí Minh
- [3]Campbell biology (10th edition),Reece J. B., Urry, L. A., Cain M. L. 1., Wasserman S. A., Minorsky P. V., Jackson R.B., & Campbell N. A.,2014,Pearson

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Understand the fundamental and modern concepts of biology.
- G2.Explain the chemical basis of life.
- G3.Distinguish the structure and explain the function of cells, biological membranes, and organelles.
- G4.Understand the material basis and mechanisms of heredity and variation.
- G5.Apply techniques of modern genetic engineering.
- G6.Understand the concepts and mechanisms of speciation, evolution, and diversity in organisms.
• Content:
- Introduction
- Basic concepts of biology.
- The Chemical Basis of Life
- Matter and chemical reactions.
- Matter and chemical reactions (continued).
- Water and life.
- Water and life (continued).
- Carbon skeletons and biological molecules.
- An overview of cell structure and function.
- The exchange of matter across cell membranes.
- Cellular metabolism processes: cellular respiration and fermentation.
- Cellular metabolism processes: cellular respiration and fermentation (continued).
- Cellular metabolism processes: photosynthesis.
- Structure of DNA, mechanisms of replication and DNA repair.
- Structure of chromosomes - Chromosome variations.

- Bacterial and viral genetics.

# COURSE: Sinh đại cương 2 (BIO00002)

## GENERAL INFORMATION
- **Course ID**: BIO00002
- **English Name**: Biology 2
- **Vietnamese Name**: Sinh đại cương 2
- **Responsible Person**: for the module:
- **Semester**: (s) in which the module is taught: 3
- **Workload**: (incl. contact hours, self-study hours): Hours for lecture: ~ 37.5 hours Hours for laboratory session: ~ 0 hours Hours for self-study, examination preparation, other activities: ~ 86.25 hours

## COURSE DESCRIPTION
• COURSE TOPICS:
- Overview of plant morphology and nutrition
- Transport in vascular plants
- Mineral nutrition in plants
- Reproduction in seed plants
- Plant responses to internal and external signals
- Hormones and the endocrine system
- The nervous system
- Nutrition in animals
- The circulatory system and gas exchange
- The immune system
- Reproduction and embryonic development in animals
- Osmoregulation and the mechanisms of sensation and movement
- Introduction to Ecology
- Population and community ecology
- Ecosystems, the biosphere, and biodiversity conservation
• COURSE GOALS: On successful completion of this course, students will be able to:
• REQUIRED AND RECOMMENDED READING:
- [1]Ecology: Individuals, Populations and Communities,Begon M., J.L. Harper, and C.R. Townsend,1986,Blackwell Scientific Publications
- [2]Biology,Brooker, R.J, Widmaier E.P; Graham L.E and Stiling P.D.,2014,McGraw-Hill
- [3]Biology (10th Edition),Campbell N.A, J.B. Reece, L.A. Urry, M.I. Cain, S.A. Wasserman, P.V. Minorsky, and R. B. Jackson,2014,Pearson, Benjamin Cummings
- [4]Ecology: Theories and Applications (Fourth Edition),Stiling P. D.,2002,Prentice-Hall, Inc.
- [5]Plant biology,Smith A.M; Coupland G; Dolan L; Harberd N; Jones J; Martin C; Sablowski R; and Amey A.,2010,Garland Science

## LEARNING OBJECTIVES (CLOs)
- G1.Acquire basic knowledge about plant morphology.
- G2.Understand the basic functional processes of plants in the life cycle.
- G3.Acquire basic knowledge about animal morphology.
- G4.Understand the basic functional processes of animals in the life cycle.
- G5.Understand the fundamental concepts of ecology.
- G6.Understand the significance of biodiversity and the role of biodiversity conservation for Vietnam and the world.
• Content:
- Overview of plant morphology and nutrition
- Transport in vascular plants
- Mineral nutrition in plants
- Reproduction in seed plants
- Plant responses to internal and external signals
- Hormones and the endocrine system
- The nervous system
- Nutrition in animals
- The circulatory system and gas exchange
- The immune system
